title: ranunculus geoides var. oaxacensis
intro: Ranunculus geoides var. oaxacensis, also known as the Mexican buttercup, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Its herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
